patterns.title: shadcn composition baseline
patterns.summary: Prefer composable shadcn primitives over custom one-off widgets when building new internal product surfaces.
when_to_use: A new screen can be assembled from existing primitives with minor styling work.
The implementation should stay aligned with the repository stack defaults.
constraints: Do not create bespoke UI components when a primitive composition solves the same problem cleanly.
Keep styling localized so future screens can reuse the pattern.
solution_outline: Start with Card, Tabs, Table, Dialog, Sheet, and form primitives before inventing new abstractions.
Extract a reusable wrapper only after two concrete usages exist.
Keep behavior code separate from cosmetic wrappers.
patterns.selected_because: Matched task terms: existing, keep, songs, publish

patterns.title: List-detail workspace shell
patterns.summary: Reusable split-pane pattern for operator flows that need fast scanning and a focused detail view.
when_to_use: The user needs to scan a list and inspect one selected item without leaving context.
A task benefits from keyboard-friendly repeat interactions and persistent selection state.
constraints: The selected item must remain visually obvious.
An empty state is required before the first selection.
The detail pane should not block scanning the list.
solution_outline: Use a two-column layout with the collection on the left and detail content on the right.
Keep selected item state close to the surface that owns the list.
Define explicit empty, loading, and selected states for the detail pane.
patterns.selected_because: Matched task terms: songs, keep, workspace, chart
